page: Microsoft Azure E64-32ads_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Eadsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor, operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16, alongside cryptography extensions like AES and SHA_NI. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per vCPU. Local storage is bundled, offering 3780 GB of local disk capacity with Premium Storage capability, while no GPUs are present.

Benchmark data reveals top-tier single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU testing, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression placing in the top 10% of tested servers. Multi-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores landing in the average and strong categories respectively. Memory performance is robust, with a PassMark memory score in the top 25%, though uncached large-block memory read and write bandwidths remain in the average tier. For specialized workloads, the server achieves top-tier text generation speeds on small LLM models, but performance drops significantly to the bottom 10% for large LLM models due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E64-32ads_v7 offers a dense allocation of memory and local storage relative to its 32 vCPUs, making it a viable option for resource-heavy workloads that do not require massive parallel compute scaling. The combination of 512 GB RAM, fast single-core execution, and substantial local storage makes this server highly suitable for large relational databases, in-memory caching layers like Redis, data warehousing, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
description: The Microsoft Azure E64-32ads_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores, it features 512.0 GB of RAM and 3780 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capabilities. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, alongside strong multi-threaded decompression and memory performance. However, multi-core scaling and large-block memory bandwidth remain average. Lacking GPU acceleration, it is poorly suited for large-scale AI model inference but excels in memory-intensive workloads. The high memory-to-vCPU density and substantial local storage make this server highly efficient for large databases, in-memory analytics, and enterprise applications requiring rapid single-core execution and extensive memory capacity.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64-32ads_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 512 GB of RAM, and 3780 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for databases.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64-32ads_v7 is a memory-optimized VM with 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage, built for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-dense virtual machine featuring high-speed single-core performance and extensive local storage for demanding database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 vCPUs operating at 4.5 GHz.
512 GB of RAM providing a high 16 GB per vCPU ratio.
3780 GB of bundled local storage with Premium Storage support.
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance.
Optimized for large databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications.
